component.name: NES Studio (Development)
component.summary: Make a real NES game, then play it

description.p: NES Studio is a game maker for schools. Pupils draw the tiles, paint the world, design the characters and choose the rules — and the Studio compiles it with cc65 into a real .nes ROM, which it then plays on an emulated console in the same window.
What it teaches, by making it visible rather than by telling you:

ul.li: The NES has 64 colours and can show four at a time. The palette editor is the real system palette, and no other.
One palette per 2x2 tile quadrant. A quadrant that uses two is flagged on the canvas, before the build, not after.
256 background tiles, 256 sprite tiles, 64 hardware sprites, 32 KB of cartridge. Live meters, so the ceiling is met as a limit and not as a crash.
Tiles are shared. Repainting one character's boot can repaint another's — so the Studio stops and asks, in those words.

description.p: Everything is checked by around thirty validators, each of which can take you to the exact mode that fixes it. Six guided tutorials advance on their own as the work is done.
